channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Notre Dame's Riley Leonard thanks fans for coming to Sugar Bowl in New Orleans after terror attack
item.description: Fans packed Caesars Superdome 36 hours after a terror attack nearly two miles away, and Riley Leonard made sure to thank the Notre Dame faithful.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rugby star Ilona Maher eyes Bristol debut as ticket sales break club record
item.description: BRISTOL, England (AP) — Ticket sales have broken a record ahead of U.S. rugby star Ilona Maher making her debut for the Bristol Bears women's team.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LeBron James applauds younger son Bryce's hoops commitment to the University of Arizona
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James is applauding the decision made by his son, Bryce, to play basketball at the University of Arizona next season.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White leads No. 22 Illinois to 109-77 blowout at No. 9 Oregon
item.description: EUGENE, Ore. (AP) — Tre White had 20 points and 11 rebounds as No. 22 Illinois made a resounding statement on the road with a 109-77 blowout of ninth-ranked Oregon on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MIMS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Utah HC storms back at Calgary to end five-game skid
item.description: CALGARY (ABC4 Sports) - Lawson Crouse scored twice, and the Utah Hockey Club rallied for a 5-3 win against the Calgary Flames at Scotiabank Saddledome on Thursday to end a five-game losing skid.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Tom Hoge leads at Kapalua where good golf exceeds expectations in PGA Tour opener
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — Tom Hoge grew up in North Dakota and found the ideal vibe for Kapalua on Thursday, keeping expectations low and riding the momentum of good golf on his way to a 9-under 64 to take a one-shot lead at The Sentry in the PGA Tour season opener.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Georgia's Kirby Smart defends aggressive but ill-fated play calls in CFP loss to Notre Dame
item.description: Georgia coach Kirby Smart says he doesn't regret aggressive offensive play calls that backfired during the Bulldogs' College Football Playoff quarterfinal loss to Notre Dame in the Sugar Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Guerry Smith, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Freeman and Notre Dame handle 'tough moments' and oust Georgia from CFP with 23-10 win in Sugar Bowl
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Notre Dame coach Marcus Freeman and the Fighting Irish found the right balance of family and football to produce a memorable performance under unprecedented, emotionally trying circumstances.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cam Skattebo sounds off on fan accusing ASU star running back of late night out before Peach Bowl
item.description: Arizona State’s Cam Skattebo fired back on a fan on social media Thursday after the star running back was accused of having a late night out before Wednesday's Peach Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big 12 commissioner calls for 'national standards' for calling targeting after controversial play
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Big 12 commissioner Brett Yormark is calling for more consistency in determining what constitutes a targeting penalty after no call was made on Texas safety Michael Taaffe's high hit on Arizona State receiver Melquan Stovall late in the College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Peac
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sugar Bowl kicks off after New Orleans terror attack
item.description: The Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame is underway more than a day after a deadly terror attack on Bourbon Street claimed the lives of at least 15 people and injured dozens more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pistons guard Jaden Ivey has surgery on leg he broke in collision vs. Magic
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit Pistons guard Jaden Ivey had surgery on his left leg, a day after breaking his fibula in a collision during a win over the Orlando Magic. The Pistons said Thursday that Ivey will be reevaluated in four weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Antetokounmpo, Jokic and James among early NBA All-Star leading vote-getters
item.description: Milwaukee's Giannis Antetokounmpo is the early leader in fan balloting for the NBA All-Star Game, and LeBron James of the Los Angeles Lakers seems well on his way toward extending his record and getting picked for a 21st time.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bears CB Jaylon Johnson earns team's lone Pro Bowl selection this season, his 2nd straight
item.description: Bears cornerback Jaylon Johnson has been selected to represent the NFC at the 2025 Pro Bowl Games, the NFL announced Thursday morning. The games will be held a week before the Super Bowl, on Sunday, Feb. 2, at Camping World Stadium in Orlando, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin aiming to be back on snow 'in the next week or so' following November crash
item.description: Mikaela Shiffrin is back in the gym and aiming to be on snow “in the next week or so,” the American skiing star said more than a month after suffering a deep puncture wound in her hip area. The serious injury occurred during a during a crash on Nov. 30 in Killington, Vermont.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ben Johnson likely to push for a GM besides Ryan Poles, report says
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Information continues to emerge about the Chicago Bears' looming pursuit of a new head coach. The presumptive top candidate — Ben Johnson — is tied to the latest details suggesting he would want a new general manager if he came to Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Westbrook's triple-double leads Denver past Utah
item.description: DENVER (AP) - Russell Westbrook became the second player in NBA history to have a triple-double with zero turnovers and shoot 100% from the field and the free-throw line in the Denver Nuggets' 132-121 win over the Utah Jazz on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs with Bowles thanks a near-perfect offensive day, and a duck, for the team's return to first place
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers returned to the top of the NFC South on Sunday with a dominating 48-14 win over the Carolina Panthers, paired with a loss by the Atlanta Falcons.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lakers trade former All-Star to Brooklyn
item.description: For the second time in his NBA career, D'Angelo Russell has been traded from the Los Angeles Lakers to the Brooklyn Nets.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5
